name: Paris
description: Paris, also known as Alexander, was a prince of Troy, son of King Priam and Queen Hecuba. Before his birth, Hecuba dreamed that she gave birth to a firebrand that consumed Troy, leading to his abandonment on Mount Ida. He grew up as a shepherd, known for his beauty and fairness. His destiny was irrevocably altered when he was chosen by Zeus to judge the beauty contest between Hera, Athena, and Aphrodite. Each goddess offered him a bribe: Hera promised him power, Athena promised him wisdom, and Aphrodite promised him the most beautiful woman in the world, Helen. Paris chose Aphrodite, thus winning Helen, who was already married to King Menelaus of Sparta. Paris's abduction of Helen sparked the Trojan War, a decade-long conflict that ultimately led to the destruction of Troy. He was a skilled archer and warrior, though often depicted as less courageous than other Trojan heroes. He killed Achilles by shooting him in the heel with an arrow, guided by Apollo. Paris was eventually wounded by Philoctetes and died, fulfilling the prophecy of his birth. His actions set in motion events of epic proportions, highlighting the themes of fate, beauty, desire, and the devastating consequences of choices. He represents both the allure of beauty and the destructive power of unchecked passion. His legacy is forever intertwined with the fall of Troy and the tragic events of the Trojan War.
subtitle: Prince of Troy, Abductor of Helen
achievements: Judged the beauty contest of the goddesses

keyMyths.title: The Judgement of Paris
keyMyths.description: Zeus tasked Paris with choosing the fairest goddess between Hera, Athena, and Aphrodite. Each offered a bribe, and Paris chose Aphrodite's promise of Helen, the most beautiful woman, leading to the Trojan War. This decision sealed Troy's doom.

keyMyths.title: The Abduction of Helen
keyMyths.description: Paris, with Aphrodite's help, abducted Helen from Sparta while Menelaus was away. This act violated the sacred laws of hospitality and sparked the Trojan War, as Menelaus sought to reclaim his wife and honor.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Achilles
keyMyths.description: Paris, guided by Apollo, shot Achilles in his vulnerable heel with an arrow, fulfilling a prophecy and ultimately leading to Achilles' death. This event significantly impacted the course of the Trojan War.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Epic poem detailing the Trojan War, featuring Paris's role in the conflict.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Greek mythology, including Paris's early life and the Judgement of Paris.
